danger_signals.meaning: Town/area under hostile occupation; threat to Elves and water supply

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental water discoloration or blocked fountains in town areas
danger_signals.meaning: Elf life source corrupted; resource scarcity; world degradation

danger_signals.visual_form: Eolis hometown shown in disrepair, abandoned structures
danger_signals.meaning: Civilization in decline; danger zone; conflict aftermath

danger_signals.visual_form: Magic projectiles from enemies, tentacle variants shooting at higher trajectories
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring avoidance or defense

danger_signals.visual_form: Interior dungeon screens with maze-like layouts (mountain stronghold, tree interior)
danger_signals.meaning: High-danger zones; increased enemy concentration; boss territory

safety_signals.visual_form: Church buildings with religious iconography (crosses, crucifixes, rosaries in Japanese version; removed in international versions)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; NPC interaction point; quest/rank progression hub

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinctive NPC portrait; priest-like robes and appearance (called 'Guru' in international versions)
safety_signals.meaning: Trustworthy guide; provider of password/mantra salvation system; rank advancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Elf king character; World Tree visual architecture; buttress and branch areas
safety_signals.meaning: Allied faction territory; quest origin; safe passage zones

safety_signals.visual_form: Blue lady NPC (ID 53, unused) and child NPC (ID 54, unused) suggest planned safe civilian zones
safety_signals.meaning: Non-combat interaction areas; information gathering; story progression
Health restoration opportunity; safe looting confirmation

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tree buttress, tree trunk interior, tree branches; Eolis town; churches with religious iconography

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mountain stronghold 'Fortress of Zenis'; dwarf king crown/regalia
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Transformed dwarf monsters (hostile variants); enemy ID 18, 29, 36, 39, 43, 70 include dwarf-type variants and tentacle enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dwarves' mountain stronghold (final dungeon); Castle of Fraternal (second-to-last dungeon)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: All dwarf monster variants are visual manifestations of Evil One's corruption; tentacle enemy represents otherworldly corruption
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Poisoned water; dilapidated environments; mountain stronghold interior; dark dungeon areas

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dragon Slayer sword (magical artifact); equipment upgrades (Battle Helmet, Battle Suit)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Overworld travel route from Eolis through tree zones to mountain stronghold; shop access; rank progression visible via GUI

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: World Tree in full health; Elves and Dwarves coexisting; fountain flowing
Poisoned water (environmental discoloration); meteorite impact visual; dwarves transformed into monster sprites with altered animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dwarves become hostile; emit projectiles; attack on sight; water sources become blocked or toxic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Town buildings in disrepair; abandoned appearance; dwarf enemy sprites present in town areas; structures damaged
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dwarves patrol town; NPCs absent or sheltering; quest-giver Elven King isolated; immediate combat threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Equipment upgrades visible on hero sprite; rank increases reflected in GUI display; treasure pickups; experience accumulation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hero can access new armor/weapons; interact with more NPCs; survive higher-damage enemy encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dwarf King Grieve visual (holding stolen Dragon Slayer sword in body); Fortress of Zenis architecture; final dungeon maze layout
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: King Grieve boss arena spans multiple screens (planned multi-screen fight); requires Dragon Slayer to defeat; magic less effective per NPC warning

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Return of Dragon Slayer to Elves (narrative goal); implied water restoration; Dwarf transformation reversal
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Quest completion; NPC dialogue confirms 'true peace' achieved; hero can no longer wear battle equipment after returning sword

environmental_storytelling.example: Eolis hometown shows disrepair and abandonment at game start, visually communicating how long the elf/dwarf conflict has lasted

environmental_storytelling.technique: NPC dialogue to expose hidden world-state
environmental_storytelling.example: Guru explains water fountain stopped and remaining water poisoned; reveals systemic life-source failure; each NPC adds lore layers about conflict origins and Evil One emergence

Journey from tree buttress → tree trunk → tree branches → mountain stronghold physically maps escalating danger and faction territory; each zone represents deeper penetration into corrupted dwarf domain
Battle Helmet and Battle Suit only equippable with Dragon Slayer in possession; NPC dialogue states Elves won't return sword until 'true peace here'—gear availability communicates political condition

environmental_storytelling.technique: Item scarcity and shop economy as world-state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Hero given 1500 gold at start to prepare; resource management through shopping reflects limited supplies during siege; enemy drops (bread, gold, items) indicate scarcity of normal trade

Churches with crosses and crucifixes (Japanese) signal safe spiritual zones; guru portraits and rosaries reinforce faith-based salvation mechanic; international removal of crosses indicates censorship of world-state messaging

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy sprite variation as corruption indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Tentacle enemy (unused variant and placed variant) represents non-dwarf corruption; multiple enemy IDs (18, 29, 36, 39, 43, 70) show Evil One's influence mutates diverse life forms beyond dwarves

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Attack on sight; emit projectiles; patrol town and dungeons; unable to recognize former allies
Swallowed Dragon Slayer sword to hide it; controlled by Evil One; multi-screen arena fight; requires non-magic primary strategy per NPC warning
Grants initial 1500 gold; explains world-state crisis; distributes quest objectives; remains in safe town area
Provide password/mantra salvation; grant rank advancement when experience thresholds met; remain in churches (safe zones)
Shoots projectiles at higher Y-axis than dwarf variants; suggests otherworldly origin distinct from dwarf transformation
Collects equipment and magic to grow stronger; interacts with NPCs for information and rank-up; actively seeks Dragon Slayer as only Dwarf-transformation cure
Transforms dwarves into monsters against will; poisons water supply; prevents natural restoration of harmony; uses Dwarf King as proxy vessel

notes: Faxanadu uses classical medieval action-RPG visual language to communicate a world in political/magical crisis. The primary manifestation mechanism is environmental degradation (poisoned water, dilapidated architecture) paired with enemy sprite variants (transformed dwarves, tentacle creatures) to signal corruption. Faction identity relies on geography (World Tree for Elves, mountain stronghold for Dwarves) and NPC placement (churches as safe Elf-aligned zones). The game's password/mantra system—tied to Gurus in religious spaces—serves as a meta-narrative signal that spiritual intervention is needed to restore balance. Regional differences (removal of crosses, crucifix, rosary iconography; priest renamed to guru) show how religious visual language was used as primary world-state signifier. Unused NPCs (blue lady, child) and unused dialogue suggest early design included more nuanced civilian impact visualization. The Dragon Slayer sword as a magic artifact that must be recovered from the Dwarf King's body is the central visual quest object symbolizing that the only path to peace is drawing the weapon that can destroy the Evil One—a stark communication that violence is the solution to corruption, not negotiation.
